text: **Speaker 1:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**Speaker 1:** Jill.
**Speaker 1:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Speaker 1:** Today we {talk|話す} about something really {important|重要な/大切な}.
**Speaker 1:** Friends.
**Speaker 1:** We have {fun|楽しい} with our friends during {good times|良い時}.
**Speaker 1:** And they help us during {bad times|悪い時}.
**Speaker 1:** But what do friends do together?
**Speaker 1:** That is the question Anna {answers|答える}.
**Speaker 1:** What do friends do together?
**Speaker 2:** Hello!
**Speaker 2:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} to my {tree house|木の家}.
Anna, our question for today is, what do friends do together?
**Speaker 2:** Well, some friends make things together.
**Speaker 2:** Today I am making a {friendship|友情} {bracelet|腕輪}.
**Speaker 2:** I want to make a {friendship|友情} bracelet.
**Speaker 2:** For me.
**Speaker 2:** Daisy, friends make {friendship|友情} bracelets for each other.
But Daisy does not have a friend.
**Speaker 2:** Max, you are her friend.
**Speaker 2:** But we {fight|戦う/争う}
**Speaker 2:** Sometimes friends {fight|戦う/争う}.
But we fight often.
**Speaker 2:** I have an idea.
**Speaker 2:** You two must make {friendship|友情} bracelets
**Speaker 2:** For each other.
**Speaker 2:** Great idea, Anna.
**Speaker 2:** I do not make things.
**Speaker 2:** Daisy, this time you must
**Speaker 1:** We do many things with our friends.

**Speaker 1:** {Friends|友人/仲間|/frendz/|My friends are very kind.} can {eat|食べる|/iːt/|We eat lunch together.} together, play {games|ゲーム/遊び|/ɡeɪmz/|We play games after school.}, or just spend time together.
**Speaker 1:** In English, we call that {hanging out|一緒に過ごす|/ˈhæŋɪŋ aʊt/|We like hanging out on weekends.}.
**Speaker 1:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**Speaker 1:** They talk about things they do with their friends.
My dad said it is {Hayacam|不明語|/hɑːjəkæm/|Unknown term}.
We like to do {organize|整理する/準備する|/ˈɔːrɡənaɪz/|Let's organize our books.} together.
**Speaker 5:** I often {help|助ける|/help/|I help my mom with dishes.} my sister with {homework|宿題|/ˈhoʊmwɜːrk/|I do my homework every day.}
**Speaker 6:** During school I {eat|食べる|/iːt/|We eat lunch together.} lunch with my friends.
**Speaker 6:** This is my {lunchbox|弁当箱|/ˈlʌntʃbɒks/|I packed my lunch in this lunchbox.}
**Speaker 1:** Thanks.
**Speaker 1:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She talks about {adverbs|副詞|/ˈædvɜːrbz/|Adverbs describe actions.}
**Speaker 1:** Adverbs have many uses.
**Speaker 1:** For example, they tell how we do something.
**Speaker 1:** They also can tell us how often actions happen.
**Speaker 1:** Look at this sentence.
**Speaker 1:** Do you see any adverbs?
**Speaker 1:** The friends {usually|通常/よく|/ˈjuːʒuəli/|I usually eat breakfast at home.} played {happily|楽しそうに/喜んで|/ˈhæpɪli/|They played happily in the park.}
**Speaker 1:** Happily and usually are adverbs.
**Speaker 1:** Happily tells how the friends played and usually tells how often.
**Speaker 1:** Great job finding adverbs!
**Speaker 1:** Now let's talk about another word.
**Speaker 1:** Must.
**Speaker 1:** Must means you have to do something.
**Speaker 1:** You do not have a choice.
**Speaker 1:** Let's go back to Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She and a friend are making {friendship|友情|/ˈfrendʃɪp/|Our friendship is strong.} bracelets together.
**Speaker 1:** Listen for the word must.
**Speaker 2:** My friend Zuri and I are going to make {friendship|友情|/ˈfrendʃɪp/|Our friendship is strong.} bracelets.
**Speaker 2:** Yay!
**Speaker 2:** This is how you make a {friendship bracelet|友情のブレスレット|/ˈfrendʃɪp ˈbreɪslət/|I made a friendship bracelet for my best friend}.
**Speaker 6:** We can {pick up|拾う/選ぶ|/pɪk ʌp/|Pick up your toys.} one of our favorite animals that we like and pick up.
**Speaker 2:** You need {beads|ビーズ|/bɪdz/|Colorful beads for a craft project}.
**Speaker 2:** The beads must have {holes|穴|/hoʊlz/|The button has two holes.}
**Speaker 2:** You also need {string|ひも/糸|/strɪŋ/|Tie the string carefully.}
**Speaker 2:** The string must fit through the hole.
**Speaker 2:** Then you put the string through the hole.
**Speaker 2:** You can spell your friend's name with beads, but you must spell it {right|正しく|/raɪt/|Write the word right.}
**Speaker 2:** Finally, you must {tie|結ぶ/縛る|/taɪ/|Tie your shoelaces.} the end of the string.
**Speaker 2:** Sara, you did a great job.
**Speaker 2:** Now let's go back to the {treehouse|ツリーハウス|/ˈtriːhaʊs/|Kids love playing in a treehouse.}
**Speaker 2:** I want to see Max and Daisy's friendship bracelets
**Speaker 6:** Good idea.
**Speaker 6:** Let's go see their friendship bracelets right now.
**Speaker 6:** Let's go.
**Speaker 2:** Daisy, what did you do?
**Speaker 2:** I made Max a friendship {chain|チェーン/鎖|/tʃeɪn/|A gold chain around my neck.}
**Speaker 2:** I said friendship bracelet, not chain.
**Speaker 2:** You must {unlock|解放する/開く|/ʌnˈlɒk/|Unlock the door with a key.} him.
**Speaker 2:** Until next time, I must help my friend.
**Speaker 2:** Hold still.
**Speaker 1:** Friends make life happy and {interesting|面白い/興味深い|/ˈɪntrəstɪŋ/|An interesting story keeps me engaged.}
**Speaker 1:** And friends also help each other.
**Speaker 1:** Use the words you learned to talk about your friends.
**Speaker 1:** Join us again next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
